title: INtopBackup
description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from INtopBackup, skipping any feeds with errors.

items.title: American speedskater Jordan Stolz wins second Olympic gold with 500-meter race victory
items.description: With the win, Stolz joins Eric Heiden as the only skaters to take gold in both the 500 and 1,000 at the same Olympics.

items.title: Obama on ICE shootings in Minnesota: "This is not the America we believe in"
items.description: President Obama spoke about the "unprecedented nature" of what he said Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents did in Minnesota.

items.title: Planned US-funded baby vaccine trial in Guinea-Bissau blasted by WHO
items.description: Giving some newborns in Guinea-Bissau an established hepatitis B treatment but not others is "unethical", it says.

items.title: No arrests made after police search residence near Nancy Guthrie's home
items.description: A search has been carried out in Tuscon, Arizona, close to where the 84-year-old was abducted nearly two weeks ago.

items.title: Gen Z is logging off and looking for love this Valentine's Day with matchmakers
items.description: Interest in matchmakers is rising as Gen Z disenchantment with dating apps grows, experts say.

items.title: Russia killed opposition leader Alexei Navalny using dart frog toxin, UK says
items.description: There is no innocent explanation for the toxin being found in samples taken from Navalny's body, Foreign Office says.

items.description: The annual aficionados' bonanza has been postponed until further notice as shortages affect international travel.

items.title: Olympic organizers promise Valentine's weekend restock after condoms run out
